message: The other night, we had a surprise blackout that left my neighborhood in darkness. I was cozy on the couch, just settling in for a movie marathon when—boom!—the power went out. My phone was charged, and I had my trusty lantern ready to go. But then there was my friend Sarah, who has a knack for panicking over the smallest things. "What are we going to do?" she exclaimed, her voice rising. I couldn't help but chuckle a bit; in moments like these, I usually feel pretty prepared. I handed her a flashlight and reminded her that we could still make popcorn on the stovetop and play board games by lantern light. It turned into a fun evening filled with laughter instead of worry. It’s moments like these that remind me how important it is to be ready for the unexpected without losing our cool. How do you handle power outages? Any fun stories to share?
